channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How Andy Burnham, ‘King of the North,’ Conquered U.K. Politics
item.description: A plain-spoken politician from a modest background in northwest England, Mr. Burnham became Labour leader — and Britain’s incoming prime minister — on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:credit: Jon Super/Associated Press
item.media:description: Andy Burnham in Ashton-in-Makerfield, England, in June. Mr. Burnham is on the verge of becoming prime minister.

item.title: New Prime Minister Faces Old Problems: How to Make Britain’s Economy Grow
item.description: Conversations with economists, and people around him, shed light on how Andy Burnham might tackle entrenched challenges.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Andy Burnham, who is set to become Britain's prime minister on Monday, hopes that the resurgence in Manchester, where he was mayor, can be replicated across the country.

item.title: What a Times Reporter Saw in Iran, and Takeaways from Trump’s Speech on Election Security
item.description: Plus, the Friday news quiz.
item.dc:creator: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Abdi Latif Dahir and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: This month, mourners gathered at the Grand Mosalla, a mosque complex in Tehran, to pay respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei during his funeral.

item.title: Trump’s Obsession With Relitigating the 2020 Election Carries a Cost for Democracy
item.description: In demanding steps to address the integrity of voting, President Trump persisted in relitigating his 2020 election defeat while finding ways to cast doubt on the 2026 outcome.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump seen on a screen inside the White House briefing room as he addressed the nation on Thursday night.

item.title: Takeaways From Trump’s Speech Claiming Election Vulnerabilities and China Interference
item.description: Documents released by the Trump administration to support the president’s claims did not back up his most aggressive statements.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Maggie Haberman
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Saul Loeb

item.title: Trump Promised Proof of Election Tampering. His Document Release Fell Far Short.
item.description: In the end, the documentary evidence that President Trump claimed would prove his case appeared bound to disappoint those who expected bombshell revelations.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Dustin Volz
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Saul Loeb
item.media:description: President Donald Trump points to attendees after delivering an address to the nation from the East Room on Thursday night.

item.title: In Prime Time, Trump Criticizes Networks for Not Carrying His Speech
item.description: The president also called for ABC and NBC to lose their broadcast licenses, after complaining that the networks were not airing his remarks.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump was set to address the nation at 9 p.m. on Thursday night.

item.title: Ontario Wildfires Burn On, and New Ones Spring Up
item.description: More than 180 fires continued to devastate swaths of the province, prompting evacuations in at least 15 communities.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:credit: Cole Burston/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Smoke was visible from the observation deck of the CN Tower in Toronto on Thursday.

item.title: Xi Jinping of China Pitches ‘Openness’ in Push to Shape the Path of A.I.
item.description: Xi Jinping said that the development of artificial intelligence systems should be a “symphony of global collaboration.”
item.dc:creator: Steve Lohr
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Ng Han Guan
item.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, spoke at the start of the World AI Conference in Shanghai on Friday.

item.title: Big Tech Firms Like Oracle Turn to Bonds to Finance A.I. Data Centers
item.description: Risk is rising as big tech companies like Oracle — the ultimate financial source of the Ellison media empire — need to turn to the bond market for staggering sums to finance data centers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer
item.media:credit: Jason Hoffman

item.title: Stocks Sink on Anxiety About Tech and A.I. Spending
item.description: A sell-off in Asia led by chipmakers spilled into Europe and U.S. markets on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:credit: Brendan Mcdermid/Reuters

item.title: Christopher Nolan on ‘The Odyssey’ and IMAX Technical Challenges
item.description: He and the cinematographer Hoyte van Hoytema combined new inventions (like fire-mimicking lights they developed) with century-old techniques.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik
item.media:credit: Erik Tanner for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I called up my friends at IMAX,” Christopher Nolan said. “I couldn’t tell them what the movie was yet, but I said if ever we were going to do a film all in IMAX, this is the one.”

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Passion in Every Frame
item.description: Few directors close the divide between the art film and the blockbuster like Christopher Nolan.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Want More of the ‘Odyssey’? Read These Books Next
item.description: Informative histories, modern retellings and genre-bending homages will take you back to the long road to Ithaca.
item.dc:creator: Calum Marsh

item.title: Dubai Migrant Worker Layoffs Reveal Costs of Middle East War
item.description: The Persian Gulf city attracted migrant workers with the promise of opportunity. Many now say the war has brought job losses and salary cuts.
item.dc:creator: Anjana Sankar and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Natalie Naccache for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Satwa area of Dubai in April. Migrant workers in the city have faced job losses or cuts since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: Cyclospora Linked to Taylor Farms Lettuce Sent to Taco Bell
item.description: Federal officials worked with Michigan investigators to trace the outbreak to iceberg lettuce, which may also have gone to other vendors.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett, Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi
item.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
item.media:description: Cyclosporiasis cases have been linked to iceberg lettuce that Taylor Farms supplied to Taco Bell.

item.title: The 20-Somethings Running Ukraine’s War Machine
item.description: A new generation has been cutting red tape to speed weapons to the front. But the old guard pushed back, and now Ukraine’s young defense minister is out.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: A friend’s call for help led Mykhailo Rudominski into defense work. His company, Himera, makes electronic warfare-resistant radios.

item.title: Ukraine Was on a Roll. Then a Clash Over War Strategy Exploded Into View.
item.description: From an underground parking garage, Ukraine’s newly dismissed defense minister aired the most dramatic, public critique of the military command to emerge during the war.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov, the former minister of defense, speaking in Kyiv on Thursday.

item.title: A Russian Political Battle Ends With a Visit From Masked Men
item.description: Boris B. Nadezhdin, an outspoken opponent of the war in Ukraine, says he knows why he was detained: “Among our leadership, there is panic and chaos.”
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Alina Lobzina
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: Boris B. Nadezhdin at home in June.

item.title: Manhattan Building’s Buckled Columns Probably Lacked Required Reinforcements
item.description: Drawings obtained by The New York Times show the columns called for additional steel plates, but engineers reviewing site photos said they appeared to be missing.
item.dc:creator: James Glanz and Simon Ducroquet
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: In One of Houston’s Oldest Latino Barrios, Mourning After ICE Shooting Mixed With Fear and Anger
item.description: After a federal agent killed Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, the grief and anger in Magnolia Park has been less visible, but no less intense.
item.dc:creator: Jazmine Ulloa
item.media:credit: Antranik Tavitian for The New York Times
item.media:description: A group of people prayed together at a memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston on Saturday.

item.title: Johan Guerrero, Maine ICE Shooting Victim, Relentlessly Pursued a Better Life, Partner Says
item.description: The partner of Johan Guerrero, who was killed by an immigration agent this week, described him as a tireless worker and doting father who would do anything for his 3-year-old daughter.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell, Jorge Valencia and Murray Carpenter
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial for Johan Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, where he was shot and killed by a federal immigration agent this week.

item.title: A Mathematical Tribute to the Soccer Ball
item.description: As the World Cup final approaches, take a moment to appreciate the beautiful symmetries of humanity’s favorite truncated icosahedron.
item.dc:creator: Siobhan Roberts

item.title: ‘Suddenly, I Was Free’: A Chinese Pastor’s Journey From Jail to the U.S.
item.description: In his first interview since being released by China, Pastor Ezra Jin Mingri details his surprise release after a direct appeal by President Trump to Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Pastor Ezra Jin Mingri at his home near Washington on Thursday. His release from jail was a rare concession by China after President Trump raised his case.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes Force a Grim Homecoming for Migrants
item.description: Mass migration, which defines modern Venezuela, has heavily marked the earthquake, too, saving many who had left, but also making their grief harder.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Liliana Figueroa, who had migrated to Brazil, returned to Venezuela after her daughter, Angelina, was killed in the earthquake.

item.title: Can Democrats Finally Understand the Latino Voter in an Era of Mass Deportations?
item.description: For decades, Democrats have misunderstood Latino voters. Trump’s deportation campaign offers a chance for them to get it right.
item.dc:creator: Andrea R. Flores
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Tam Stockton for The New York Times

item.title: Why Trump Can’t Leave the Smithsonian Alone
item.description: The Trump White House and its history police want us all to live in their chosen bubble, immune from instability or perceived disorder.
item.dc:creator: David W. Blight

item.title: Greek Life
item.description: We look at the place of Homer’s epic “Odyssey” in our cultural lives.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton

item.title: Late Night Is Amused by Alleged White House Teleprompter Grift
item.description: Hosts responded on Thursday to a teleprompter operator reportedly winning around $100,000 betting on what President Trump would say in his speeches.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Two Dead and Scores Rescued as Flooding Engulfs Central Texas
item.description: Flooding from relentless rain, which Gov. Greg Abbott said was likely to break records, left a trail of damage in many communities in the Hill Country region.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin, Pooja Salhotra, Edgar Sandoval, Saile Aranda and Judson Jones
item.media:credit: Christopher Lee for The New York Times
